actor_notes: Board of directors overseeing strategic alternatives and merger review.
first_evidence_text: The board frequently reviews, with the Company’s management and with the assistance of financial and legal advisors, the Company’s strategic and financial alternatives in light of developments in the Company’s business, in the sectors in which it competes, in the economy generally and in financial markets.

actor_notes: Board subcommittee authorized to oversee the sale exploration process.
first_evidence_text: The board also established an ad hoc advisory subcommittee (the “ad hoc committee”) with respect to, among other things, the review of alternatives.

actor_notes: External legal advisor to the Company.
first_evidence_text: Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz (“Wachtell Lipton”), its legal advisor

actor_notes: Financial advisor retained by the Company for the strategic review and sale process.
first_evidence_text: In July, after interviewing several potential financial advisors, the Company retained J.P. Morgan as financial advisor based on, among other factors, J.P. Morgan’s reputation, experience in mergers and acquisitions, valuation, financing and capital markets and its familiarity with the Company.

actor_notes: Stockholder advocating for a sale process and later discussing possible rollover participation.
first_evidence_text: Also during May 2014, representatives of Longview met with certain members of the board and senior management.

actor_notes: Activist stockholder publicly advocating that the Company pursue a sale.
first_evidence_text: On July 3, 2014, JANA Partners filed a Schedule 13D with the SEC disclosing that JANA had acquired 9.9% of the Company’s outstanding common stock and that JANA intended to engage in discussions with management and the board with respect to a review of strategic alternatives, including a sale of the Company.

actor_notes: Privately held industry peer evaluated as a possible combination partner but not invited into the later sale process.
first_evidence_text: During the first calendar quarter of 2014, one of the alternatives considered by the board was a merger with or acquisition of another participant in PetSmart’s industry, a privately-held company which we refer to here as “Industry Participant.”

actor_notes: Named bidder group in the late-stage sale process; constituent members are not identified in the bookmarked chronology.
first_evidence_text: Three bidders initially indicated price ranges that reached at least $80.00 per share, including the Buyer Group

actor_notes: Named late-stage bidder that increased its indication after follow-up discussions with J.P. Morgan.
first_evidence_text: another bidder (which we refer to as “Bidder 2”)

actor_notes: Joint label for two separate bidders that were authorized to work together in the final round.
first_evidence_text: We refer to these two bidders together as “Bidder 3.”

actor_notes: Placeholder for one of the remaining unidentified financial buyers that signed confidentiality and standstill agreements in the first week of October 2014.
first_evidence_text: In the first week of October 2014, the Company entered into confidentiality and standstill agreements with 15 potentially interested financial buyers
